query: Tell me about the v1.0 Public Launch task.
answer:
The task **v1.0 Public Launch** [task:148] is about shipping the redesigned marketing site to `genos.app/`.

Here are the key details:
*   **Goal**: To ship the redesigned marketing site for the v1.0 public launch, ensuring it passes accessibility audits and meets performance budgets [task:148].
*   **Success criteria**:
    *   All four marketing pages (home, pricing, about, contact) must use only `@genos/design-system v3` [task:148].
    *   Each page must have a Lighthouse mobile score of 95 or higher [task:148].
    *   There should be zero Critical or Serious violations reported by `axe-core` [task:148].
    *   Plausible event tracking for the hero CTA, pricing toggle, and contact form submit must be live [task:148].
*   **Scope**: The project includes replacing Bootstrap with the new design system, rebuilding the hero section, feature grid, and pricing table, and adding Plausible analytics. The blog page redesign, internationalization, and dark mode are out of scope for this launch [task:148].
*   **Risks**: The main dependency is the hero illustration from Carol. The backup plan is to use a placeholder gradient and add the illustration in a subsequent update [task:148].

There is a related task, **Migrate marketing pages to design system v3** [task:149], which details the technical work of replacing the old Bootstrap-based pages with the new design system components.

🧾 Summary Replace the legacy Bootstrap-based marketing pages with primitives from @genos/design-system v3. Covers the home, pricing, about, and contact pages. 🪜 Motivation Current pages stack 2019-era CSS overrides on Bootstrap 4. They fail axe-core with two Critical issues (insufficient contrast, missing landmark roles) and Lighthouse mobile tops out at 71 because of unused CSS shipped from the Bootstrap bundle. The redesign also blocks our launch — marketing wants to send traffic to the new pages first. ✅ Acceptance criteria All four pages render with @genos/design-system v3 primitives only. Lighthouse mobile score ≥ 95 on each page. axe-core reports zero Critical or Serious violations. Visual regression suite (Chromatic) passes for desktop + mobile + iPad breakpoints. 🎯 Notes & links Hero illustration handoff from Carol is in the Figma file “Marketing v3 hero”. Subtasks below break this down into audit + build phases. Coordinate Plausible analytics work (separate task) to land before the public DNS cutover.
</workspace_content>

query: Find the task about responsive navigation and tell me what its acceptance criteria say.
answer:
The acceptance criteria for the "Implement responsive navigation with mobile drawer" task [task:152] are:

*   A single `Nav` component renders correctly at 320px, 768px, 1024px, and 1440px breakpoints.
*   The drawer traps focus when open and restores focus to the toggle on close.
*   `Escape` closes the drawer.
*   The skip-to-content link is the first focusable element.

matches.title: Implement responsive navigation with mobile drawer
matches.snippet:
<workspace_content>
Implement responsive navigation with mobile drawer 🧾 Summary Replace the existing nav with a responsive component that uses a hamburger drawer below 768px and a full horizontal layout above. Keyboard focus order and screen-reader landmark must be correct. 🪜 Motivation Current...
</workspace_content>

chunks.text:
<workspace_content>
Implement responsive navigation with mobile drawer
🧾 Summary Replace the existing nav with a responsive component that uses a hamburger drawer below 768px and a full horizontal layout above. Keyboard focus order and screen-reader landmark must be correct. 🪜 Motivation Current nav is two separate components — one rendered on desktop, one on mobile — that drift from each other constantly. Six bugs this quarter have been mobile-only or desktop-only nav regressions. Folding them into one responsive component eliminates that drift. ✅ Acceptance criteria Single Nav component renders correctly at 320px, 768px, 1024px, and 1440px breakpoints. Drawer traps focus when open and restores focus to the toggle on close. Escape closes the drawer. Skip-to-content link is the first focusable element.
</workspace_content>

<workspace_content>
Initial PR is up — drawer animation feels off below 320px (very small screens). Will tune the spring config.
</workspace_content>

<workspace_content>
Add a Storybook story for the 320px case so we don't regress it. Cypress can also pick it up if we run viewport tests.
</workspace_content>

chunks.text:

<workspace_content>
Build Hero + FeatureGrid in design system v3
🧾 Summary Implement Hero and FeatureGrid components in @genos/design-system v3 with Storybook coverage. These two compose the homepage rebuild and are reused across the pricing and about pages. ✅ Acceptance criteria Hero supports image, video, and illustration variants via a single `media` prop. FeatureGrid is responsive 1 / 2 / 3 columns without per-component media queries — uses CSS container queries via design-system tokens. Storybook stories cover every variant plus the loading and empty states. axe-core passes on every Storybook story. 🎯 Notes & links Hero is mostly done — Carol's illustration is wired in behind a feature flag while the team reviews. FeatureGrid still needs the 3-column → 2-column container-query breakpoint sorted out for the iPad landscape case.
</workspace_content>

query: What do our docs say about quantum computing?
answer:
I couldn't find any internal docs mentioning quantum computing.

Based on a web search, here's a brief overview:

Quantum computing is a field of computer science that uses the principles of quantum mechanics to perform calculations. Unlike classical computers that use bits (0s or 1s), quantum computers use qubits, which can be in a superposition of both 0 and 1 simultaneously. This allows them to solve certain complex problems much faster than classical computers. [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_computing, https://www.ibm.com/quantum-computing/what-is-quantum-computing/, https://aws.amazon.com/what-is/quantum-computing/, https://www.quantinuum.com/quantum-computing]

matches.title: How to get the most out of Spotlight (Cmd-K)
matches.snippet:
<workspace_content>
🎯 Patterns that don't work well — Asking about things outside this workspace (other companies, the open web, current events) — Spotlight only knows what's in here. Math, code generation, or anything that doesn't reduce to “retrieve and summarize.”
</workspace_content>

chunks.text:
<workspace_content>
How to get the most out of Spotlight (Cmd-K)
🎯 Patterns that don't work well
Asking about things outside this workspace (other companies, the open web, current events) — Spotlight only knows what's in here.
Math, code generation, or anything that doesn't reduce to “retrieve and summarize.”
</workspace_content>

<workspace_content>
How to get the most out of Spotlight (Cmd-K)
✨ Patterns that work well
Ask about decisions: “Why did we choose CSS animations over framer-motion?” — the answer pulls Bob's DM and the spike task.
Ask about state: “What's blocking the homepage rebuild?” — pulls task statuses + comment threads.
Ask for summaries: “Summarize the customer interview signal” — pulls Alice's DMs, the PM channel thread, and the synthesis task.
Follow up: each answer keeps conversation context, so you can ask “and what's the next step?” and it knows what “the next step” refers to.
</workspace_content>

matches.title: Customer interview synthesis methodology
matches.snippet:
<workspace_content>
2. Code with the four-axis taxonomy — Jobs — what is the user trying to accomplish? Pains — what hurts in their current workflow? Workarounds — what have they hacked together to cope? Deal-breakers — what would make them switch products?

chunks.text:
<workspace_content>
Customer interview synthesis methodology
2. Code with the four-axis taxonomy
Jobs — what is the user trying to accomplish?
Pains — what hurts in their current workflow?
Workarounds — what have they hacked together to cope?
Deal-breakers — what would make them switch products?
</workspace_content>

<workspace_content>
Customer interview synthesis methodology
1. Transcribe + clean
Dovetail auto-transcription, then a human pass on every 5th paragraph (accents and overlapping speech are unreliable).
Strip filler words (“um”, “like”, “you know”) — they get in the way of theme detection.
Anonymize per the consent form. Replace company names with their industry vertical.
</workspace_content>

query: What's going on?
answer:
Based on recent activity, here’s a snapshot of what’s happening:

A major topic of discussion is the **Q3 roadmap**, which will be based on customer interview synthesis. The key decision is whether to focus on proactive surfacing (an assistant that tells you what you missed) or improved search [chat:dm:28:thread:6]. The decision is deferred until more interview transcripts can be coded [note:chat:9].

Key workstreams and blockers:
*   **Roadmap proposal**: The draft is planned to start in Sprint 2 [chat:pm:16] but is currently blocked on the customer interview synthesis, which is being led by Alice [chat:gm:8]. The proposal needs to be ready for leadership review in 3 weeks [chat:pm:16].
*   **Website Redesign**: A responsive navigation PR is up for review [chat:gm:8]. The team has decided to use pure CSS for animations to keep the bundle size small [note:chat:9].
*   **A11y audit**: Dan is blocked from starting the audit until the `FeatureGrid` PR lands [note:chat:9].

Upcoming checkpoints from the Sprint 1 kickoff recap [note:chat:9]:
*   **Friday**: A demo of the about-page migration.
*   **Next Friday**: A decision on the proactive surfacing vs. search feature.
*   **End of Sprint 1**: The marketing PR train and Plausible event PR are expected to be complete.

matches.title: Alice Chen
matches.snippet:

And what's the call if synthesis lands ambiguous?
</workspace_content>

chunks.text:
<workspace_content>

query: What's the perf budget for the marketing site, and who owns the framer-motion spike?
answer:
Based on my search, here's the information I found about the performance budget and the framer-motion spike:

*   **Performance Budget**: The marketing site has a performance budget of a **Lighthouse mobile score of 95 or higher** and a total **JavaScript bundle size of 120KB (gzipped)** [chat:dm:29:thread:4, task:148].

*   **Framer-Motion Spike**: The spike task was to investigate using `framer-motion` versus native CSS animations for the site redesign [task:156]. The team decided to use **CSS-only animations** because `framer-motion`'s 40KB gzipped bundle size was considered too large, consuming a third of the total JavaScript budget [chat:dm:29:thread:4, note:chat:9]. The investigation concluded that CSS was sufficient for the planned animations [task:156].

sources.title: Spike: framer-motion vs native CSS animations for the redesign
Migrate marketing pages to design system v3

arguments.query: marketing site performance budget and framer-motion spike owner

matches.title: Spike: framer-motion vs native CSS animations for the redesign
matches.snippet:
<workspace_content>
Spike: framer-motion vs native CSS animations for the redesign ❓ Question Should the redesign use framer-motion for the hero / drawer / modal transitions, or rely on CSS animations and Web Animations API? 💡 Hypothesis framer-motion ergonomics are better, but its 40KB gzip...
</workspace_content>

chunks.text:
<workspace_content>
Spike: framer-motion vs native CSS animations for the redesign
❓ Question Should the redesign use framer-motion for the hero / drawer / modal transitions, or rely on CSS animations and Web Animations API? 💡 Hypothesis framer-motion ergonomics are better, but its 40KB gzip footprint competes directly with our marketing-page perf budget. CSS-only may be sufficient for the animations we actually have planned. 🧭 Approach Audit the planned animations: drawer slide, modal fade, hero stagger. Inventory whether each is reachable in pure CSS. Prototype the hardest one (hero stagger) with both approaches and compare ergonomics + bundle impact. Check what other teams shipping with @genos/design-system are doing — keep us aligned. ⏱ Timebox Two days. Decision needed before the nav PR lands since the drawer animation is the first concrete usage. 📌 Findings Will write up after the prototype lands. 🚧 Out of scope In-app animations (sidebar transitions, etc.) — separate decision; this spike is marketing-page-scoped. GSAP — already ruled out for licensing reasons.
</workspace_content>

<workspace_content>
Confirmed — my prototype was 800 bytes vs framer-motion's 40KB. For marketing pages, no contest.
</workspace_content>

<workspace_content>
Leaning toward CSS-only for marketing. The drawer is the only animation that's actually tricky in pure CSS, and Bob already has a Web Animations API prototype that handles it.
</workspace_content>

chunks.text:
<workspace_content>
v1.0 Public Launch
🎯 Goal Ship the redesigned marketing site to genos.app/ for the v1.0 public launch. The new pages must pass our accessibility audit and stay under our Lighthouse mobile performance budget. ✅ Success criteria All four marketing pages (home, pricing, about, contact) render with @genos/design-system v3 only — zero Bootstrap imports remaining. Lighthouse mobile score ≥ 95 on each page. axe-core reports zero Critical or Serious violations. Plausible event tracking live for hero CTA, pricing toggle, and contact form submit. 📦 In scope Replace Bootstrap layout primitives with design-system Grid / Stack. Rebuild the hero, feature grid, and pricing table from scratch. Add Plausible analytics with named events for the CTAs above. 🚫 Out of scope Blog page redesign (deferred to v1.1). Internationalization — English only for the launch. Dark mode for the marketing site (in-app dark mode is separate). ⚠️ Risks & dependencies Hero illustration handoff from Carol is the long-pole — without it the homepage rebuild blocks. Backup plan: ship with a placeholder gradient behind the headline and swap the illustration in for v1.0.1.
</workspace_content>

Migrate marketing pages to design system v3 🧾 Summary Replace the legacy Bootstrap-based marketing pages with primitives from @genos/design-system v3. Covers the home, pricing, about, and contact pages. 🪜 Motivation Current pages stack 2019-era CSS overrides on Bootstrap 4. They...
</workspace_content>

chunks.text:
<workspace_content>
Migrate marketing pages to design system v3
🧾 Summary Replace the legacy Bootstrap-based marketing pages with primitives from @genos/design-system v3. Covers the home, pricing, about, and contact pages. 🪜 Motivation Current pages stack 2019-era CSS overrides on Bootstrap 4. They fail axe-core with two Critical issues (insufficient contrast, missing landmark roles) and Lighthouse mobile tops out at 71 because of unused CSS shipped from the Bootstrap bundle. The redesign also blocks our launch — marketing wants to send traffic to the new pages first. ✅ Acceptance criteria All four pages render with @genos/design-system v3 primitives only. Lighthouse mobile score ≥ 95 on each page. axe-core reports zero Critical or Serious violations. Visual regression suite (Chromatic) passes for desktop + mobile + iPad breakpoints. 🎯 Notes & links Hero illustration handoff from Carol is in the Figma file “Marketing v3 hero”. Subtasks below break this down into audit + build phases. Coordinate Plausible analytics work (separate task) to land before the public DNS cutover.
</workspace_content>

<workspace_content>
Hero illustration is in Figma now: file “Marketing v3 hero”. Two variants attached — gradient + photo. Lean toward the gradient one for performance.
</workspace_content>

<workspace_content>
Kicking this off. I'll start by auditing what's still using Bootstrap so we know the surface area.
</workspace_content>

matches.title: Audit existing marketing pages
matches.snippet:
<workspace_content>
Audit finished. Pricing is the messiest piece — recommend rewriting that component wholesale rather than trying to port the existing markup.
</workspace_content>

chunks.text:
<workspace_content>
Audit finished. Pricing is the messiest piece — recommend rewriting that component wholesale rather than trying to port the existing markup.
</workspace_content>

<workspace_content>
Audit existing marketing pages
🧾 Summary Inventory every marketing page and list each component still depending on Bootstrap. Output drives the migration order. 📌 Findings Homepage: hero, feature grid, and testimonials — all on Bootstrap rows. Pricing: custom Bootstrap grid override (brittle, recommend full rewrite). About: mostly plain HTML; minimal Bootstrap dependency. Lowest effort to migrate. Contact: form uses Bootstrap form-group classes plus jQuery validation — needs design-system Form primitives. 🎯 Notes & links Migration order recommendation: About → Contact → Pricing → Homepage. About is lowest-risk; homepage is highest-impact but should land after the smaller pages prove the pattern.
</workspace_content>

<workspace_content>
Agreed. The grid override there is the root cause of the cents-alignment bug we keep getting reports about. A clean rewrite kills two birds.
</workspace_content>

query: What's blocking the homepage rebuild and what's blocking the roadmap proposal?
answer:
Based on the information in your workspace, here are the blockers for the homepage rebuild and the roadmap proposal.

For the **Homepage Rebuild**:

*   **FeatureGrid component**: The responsive layout for the `FeatureGrid` component needs to be fixed for iPad landscape viewports [task:151].
*   **Hero illustration**: The rebuild is waiting on the final hero illustration from Carol [task:148].

For the **Roadmap Proposal**:

*   **Evidence synthesis**: The v1 draft is on hold until the research "synthesis" is complete to provide data for the proposal [task:162]. This synthesis includes interview themes and a competitor teardown [chat:pm:16].

matches.title: Build Hero + FeatureGrid in design system v3
matches.snippet:
<workspace_content>
Build Hero + FeatureGrid in design system v3 🧾 Summary Implement Hero and FeatureGrid components in @genos/design-system v3 with Storybook coverage. These two compose the homepage rebuild and are reused across the pricing and about pages. ✅ Acceptance criteria Hero supports...
</workspace_content>

chunks.text:
<workspace_content>
Build Hero + FeatureGrid in design system v3
🧾 Summary Implement Hero and FeatureGrid components in @genos/design-system v3 with Storybook coverage. These two compose the homepage rebuild and are reused across the pricing and about pages. ✅ Acceptance criteria Hero supports image, video, and illustration variants via a single `media` prop. FeatureGrid is responsive 1 / 2 / 3 columns without per-component media queries — uses CSS container queries via design-system tokens. Storybook stories cover every variant plus the loading and empty states. axe-core passes on every Storybook story. 🎯 Notes & links Hero is mostly done — Carol's illustration is wired in behind a feature flag while the team reviews. FeatureGrid still needs the 3-column → 2-column container-query breakpoint sorted out for the iPad landscape case.
</workspace_content>

Order will change as synthesis lands.

matches.title: Investigate slow homepage load on mobile Safari
matches.snippet:
<workspace_content>
Investigate slow homepage load on mobile Safari 🐞 Summary Reports of 4-6s homepage load times specifically on iOS Safari 16 and 17. Same network, Chrome on the same device loads in ~1s. 🔁 Steps to reproduce Open Safari on iPhone running iOS 16.5 or later. Navigate to...
</workspace_content>

chunks.text:
<workspace_content>
Investigate slow homepage load on mobile Safari
🐞 Summary Reports of 4-6s homepage load times specifically on iOS Safari 16 and 17. Same network, Chrome on the same device loads in ~1s. 🔁 Steps to reproduce Open Safari on iPhone running iOS 16.5 or later. Navigate to https://genos.app/ on a 4G connection. Observe the time-to-interactive in Safari's web inspector. 🎯 Expected behavior Homepage should be interactive in under 2.5s on mobile, matching what Chrome on the same device shows. 💥 Actual behavior Time to interactive lands at 4.2–6.1s. The waterfall shows a long blocking task during parse — likely related to the legacy Bootstrap CSS still being shipped. Memory usage also spikes well above the budget. 🧪 Environment Safari 16.5 and 17.0 on iPhone 12 and iPhone 14. 4G network throttled in Safari dev tools. Confirmed reproducible on staging and production.
</workspace_content>

<workspace_content>
Initial profiling points at the Bootstrap CSS bundle. Once we land the design-system migration this likely fixes itself, but we should confirm with a perf trace after the cutover.
</workspace_content>

<workspace_content>
QA can rerun the trace on the staging build once the migration is in.
</workspace_content>

chunks.text:
<workspace_content>
Audit existing marketing pages
🧾 Summary Inventory every marketing page and list each component still depending on Bootstrap. Output drives the migration order. 📌 Findings Homepage: hero, feature grid, and testimonials — all on Bootstrap rows. Pricing: custom Bootstrap grid override (brittle, recommend full rewrite). About: mostly plain HTML; minimal Bootstrap dependency. Lowest effort to migrate. Contact: form uses Bootstrap form-group classes plus jQuery validation — needs design-system Form primitives. 🎯 Notes & links Migration order recommendation: About → Contact → Pricing → Homepage. About is lowest-risk; homepage is highest-impact but should land after the smaller pages prove the pattern.
</workspace_content>

matches.snippet:
<workspace_content>
v1.0 Public Launch 🎯 Goal Ship the redesigned marketing site to genos.app/ for the v1.0 public launch. The new pages must pass our accessibility audit and stay under our Lighthouse mobile performance budget. ✅ Success criteria All four marketing pages (home, pricing, about,...
</workspace_content>

chunks.text:
<workspace_content>
v1.0 Public Launch
🎯 Goal Ship the redesigned marketing site to genos.app/ for the v1.0 public launch. The new pages must pass our accessibility audit and stay under our Lighthouse mobile performance budget. ✅ Success criteria All four marketing pages (home, pricing, about, contact) render with @genos/design-system v3 only — zero Bootstrap imports remaining. Lighthouse mobile score ≥ 95 on each page. axe-core reports zero Critical or Serious violations. Plausible event tracking live for hero CTA, pricing toggle, and contact form submit. 📦 In scope Replace Bootstrap layout primitives with design-system Grid / Stack. Rebuild the hero, feature grid, and pricing table from scratch. Add Plausible analytics with named events for the CTAs above. 🚫 Out of scope Blog page redesign (deferred to v1.1). Internationalization — English only for the launch. Dark mode for the marketing site (in-app dark mode is separate). ⚠️ Risks & dependencies Hero illustration handoff from Carol is the long-pole — without it the homepage rebuild blocks. Backup plan: ship with a placeholder gradient behind the headline and swap the illustration in for v1.0.1.
</workspace_content>

query: Read me the 'Spotlight tips' note in full.
answer:
Here is the full text of the "Spotlight tips" note [note:personal:28]:

### 🤖 How to get the most out of Spotlight (Cmd-K)

Spotlight is the AI search and Q&A overlay. It's not just a fuzzy file finder — it indexes every chat message, task description, comment, and note in this workspace, then routes natural-language questions through an LLM that cites the actual sources it pulled from.

### ✨ Patterns that work well

*   **Ask about decisions**: “Why did we choose CSS animations over framer-motion?” — the answer pulls Bob's DM and the spike task.
*   **Ask about state**: “What's blocking the homepage rebuild?” — pulls task statuses + comment threads.
*   **Ask for summaries**: “Summarize the customer interview signal” — pulls Alice's DMs, the PM channel thread, and the synthesis task.
*   **Follow up**: each answer keeps conversation context, so you can ask “and what's the next step?” and it knows what “the next step” refers to.

### 🎯 Patterns that don't work well

*   Asking about things outside this workspace (other companies, the open web, current events) — Spotlight only knows what's in here.
*   Math, code generation, or anything that doesn't reduce to “retrieve and summarize.”

matches.title: Customer interview synthesis methodology
matches.snippet:
<workspace_content>
📝 Common pitfalls — Collapsing themes too early — “notifications + search” is two themes, not one, until the evidence proves otherwise. Cherry-picking quotes — every theme needs a count, not just a representative quote. Recency bias — coding the latest interviews first inflates...
</workspace_content>

chunks.text:
<workspace_content>
Customer interview synthesis methodology
📝 Common pitfalls
Collapsing themes too early — “notifications + search” is two themes, not one, until the evidence proves otherwise.
Cherry-picking quotes — every theme needs a count, not just a representative quote.
Recency bias — coding the latest interviews first inflates their weight. Code in interview order.
</workspace_content>

judge.notes: The answer perfectly reproduces the full text of the requested note, as found in the tool results.
